fieldHelp.answerDescription: Add an optional explanation for stewardship. This text does not affect filtering or reports.
fieldHelp.answerPackages: Link the answer to one or more requirement packages. When the answer is selected, published requirements from those packages are included.
fieldHelp.answerText: Write the answer option that users choose in the specification.
fieldHelp.area: Choose the requirement area that owns the question. The question is grouped under the same area in the specification and receives its selection-question ID from the area prefix.
fieldHelp.questionHelpText: Optional help text shown with the question in the specification. Use it for brief guidance on how to answer.
fieldHelp.questionText: Write the question that users answer in the specification.
fieldHelp.requirementIds: Search and add published requirements by visible Requirement ID or requirement text. The answer stores requirement links behind the scenes.
fieldHelp.selectionType: Single choice allows one answer. Multiple choice allows several answers. No requirement selection is always exclusive.
fieldHelp.visibilityAnswers: Choose the parent answers that make this condition true. At least one selected answer for the parent question is enough.
fieldHelp.visibilityParent: Choose the parent requirement selection question that must have one of the selected answers.

requirementSelectionQuestionsStewardship.hierarchyDialogIntro: The lines show which requirement selection questions control other questions through visibility conditions.

privacyDataExport.title: Personal data export

requirement.title: Requirements Library
requirement.riskLevelHelp: Select the risk level for this requirement. The risk level indicates the potential impact if the requirement is not met.
requirement.verificationMethodHelp: Specify how the acceptance criteria will be verified. Choose or describe an appropriate method, such as inspection, analysis/simulation, demonstration, or test, in line with ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
requirement.areaHelp: Select the requirement area this requirement belongs to. The requirement area controls the requirement ID prefix and links the requirement to a responsible owner.
requirement.areaHelpOptional: Requirement areas are only used for requirements in the Requirements Library. Unique requirements stay within their specification until they are graduated to the Requirements Library.
requirement.descriptionHelp: Formulate the requirement clearly, concretely, and verifiably. Describe who or what the requirement applies to and what should be achieved. Focus on what should apply, not how the solution should be implemented. Where relevant, specify measurable conditions such as time, threshold, precision, or capacity so the requirement can be understood and verified. Avoid vague words like fast, simple, appropriate, or as needed. This follows the principles in ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 for clear and unambiguous requirements and ISO/IEC 25030:2019 for measurable quality requirements. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person. Examples: Good: The system shall display search results within 2 seconds. Less good: The system should be fast and user-friendly.
requirement.acceptanceCriteriaHelp: Specify the objective conditions that must be met for the requirement to be accepted. Write clearly, concretely, and measurably where relevant, such as time, threshold, precision, capacity, or observable outcome. Describe what should be fulfilled, not how it should be tested. The formulation should follow the principles in ISO/IEC 25010:2023, ISO/IEC 25030:2019, and ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
requirement.categoryHelp: Select the category that best describes the nature of the requirement. The category helps classify and filter requirements.
requirement.typeHelp: Select the type that best describes the requirement. The type determines which quality characteristics can be linked to the requirement.
requirement.qualityCharacteristicHelp: Select the quality characteristic the requirement contributes to. Quality characteristics follow ISO/IEC 25010:2023 and help categorize requirements by quality aspect.
requirement.requiresTestingHelp: Indicate whether the requirement should be verified. If the requirement is marked as verifiable, you also need to specify a verification method.
requirement.hasPendingVersionDraft: A new version is being worked on
requirement.hasPendingVersionReview: There is a newer version under review
requirement.archiveConfirm: Are you sure you want to archive this requirement?
requirement.archiveInitiateConfirm: Are you sure you want to initiate the archiving review for this requirement?
requirement.approveArchivingConfirm: Are you sure you want to archive this requirement? This action is final.
requirement.approveArchivingTooltip: Approve the archiving of this requirement
requirement.cancelArchivingConfirm: Are you sure you want to cancel the archiving process and return to Published?
requirement.cancelArchivingTooltip: Cancel archiving and return to Published status
requirement.archiveBlockedByPendingWork: Cannot archive while there is a pending draft or review version
requirement.editBlockedByPendingWork: Cannot edit while there is a pending draft or review version
requirement.restoreBlockedByPendingWork: Cannot restore while there is a pending draft or review version
requirement.deleteDraftConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this draft? The draft will be permanently removed.
requirement.deleteDraftCascadeWarning: This will also delete the requirement and {count, plural, one {# linked improvement suggestion} other {# linked improvement suggestions}}. This cannot be undone.
requirement.deleteDraftSuggestionCheckFailed: Linked improvement suggestions could not be checked. The draft was not deleted.
requirement.publishConfirm: Are you sure you want to publish this requirement? Please confirm that it has gone through the proper review process before making it available.
requirement.reactivateConfirm: Are you sure you want to restore this requirement? A new draft version will be created.
requirement.sendBackToDraftConfirm: Are you sure you want to return to a draft? You will be able to edit the requirement after.
requirement.transitionFailed: The status transition failed. Please try again.
requirement.restoreConfirm: Are you sure you want to restore this version? A new draft version will be created with data from the selected version. The requirement will need to go through the review process before it can be published.
requirement.reviewVersionAvailableBanner: A version in review (v{version}) is available
requirement.noPublishedVersion: There is no published version of this requirement.
requirement.readOnlyNotice: You can read this requirement, but you cannot change its lifecycle or content.
requirement.reviewReportAllMustBeReview: All selected requirements must be in Review status to generate the report
requirement.editNotAllowedStatusReview: This requirement cannot be edited because it is in Review status. Return it to Draft status first.
requirement.editNotAllowedStatusArchived: This requirement is Archived and must be restored before it can be edited.
requirement.editPublishedVersionNotice: This requirement is Published. Saving will create a new Draft version — the published requirement version stays in effect until the new draft completes the review process and is published.
requirement.staleEditConflict: This requirement changed while you were editing.
requirement.staleEditConflictHelp: Your changes are still here. Open the latest saved version to compare, or reload the editor before trying again.
requirement.name: Name
requirement.normReferencesHelp: Select one or more norm references governing this requirement. Norm references are for example governing laws, regulations, standards, and guidelines that the requirement is derived from or must comply with.
requirement.newNormReferenceWarning: The norm reference you create is shared and available to all requirements in the system. Make sure you enter a correct and official norm reference.
requirement.specificationLocalTooltip: This row is a unique requirement. It exists only in this specification and does not appear in the requirements library.
requirement.requirementPackageHelp: Select one or more requirements packages that apply to this requirement. Requirements packages group requirements for a specific area of concern, such as mobile use, data migration, integrations with other systems, case management, usability, cloud operations, normal operations, high load, or disaster recovery.

area.name: Name
area.changeOwnerDescription: Enter the HSA-id that should own this requirement area from now on.
area.ownerChangeCoAuthorConflict: The requirement area owner cannot also be requirement area co-author.
area.coAuthorsHelp: Add verified people who can author requirements in this requirement area.
area.coAuthorHsaIdHelp: Enter the co-author HSA-id with two uppercase country-code letters, a 10-digit organization number, a hyphen, and an alphanumeric suffix.
area.loadCoAuthorsFailed: Could not load requirement area co-authors.
area.saveCoAuthorsFailed: Could not save requirement area co-authors.

help.prefix: Enter the short uppercase prefix used when requirement IDs are generated for this requirement area. The prefix can only be changed while the requirement area has no requirements.
help.name: Enter the display name for this requirement area.
help.description: Optionally describe what kinds of requirements belong in this requirement area. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
help.owner: Enter the HSA-id for the person responsible for this requirement area. In edit mode the HSA-id is read-only; use the change-owner button to switch owner.
help.currentOwner: Shows the HSA-id currently assigned to the requirement area.
help.newOwner: Enter the replacement HSA-id. The value must use the HSA-id format and differ from the previous owner.

area.emptyState: No requirement areas yet. Create your first requirement area.

specification.name: Name

help.name: Provide a clear name that makes the specification easy to recognize and reuse.

specification.governanceObjectTypeHelp: Choose the governance object type the specification belongs to, or leave it blank if the specification spans multiple governance object types.
specification.responsibleDisplayNameHelp: Enter the display name for the specification lead. Do not use this for the user who registers a deviation unless that person is also the specification lead.
specification.responsibleHsaIdHelp: The specification lead is resolved by HSA-id. When creating a specification, the field is locked to the signed-in user; when editing, use the change button to switch lead.
specification.responsiblePairRequired: Enter both specification lead name and specification lead HSA-id, or leave both empty.
specification.invalidResponsibleHsaId: Check the specification lead HSA-id. The format is two uppercase country-code letters, 10 digits, a hyphen, and an alphanumeric suffix.
specification.currentUserUnavailable: Could not read the signed-in user's HSA-id. A requirements specification cannot be created until an HSA-id is available.
specification.changeResponsibleDescription: Change the specification lead by entering and verifying a new HSA-id.
specification.currentResponsibleHsaIdHelp: Shows the HSA-id currently assigned to this specification.
specification.newResponsibleHsaIdHelp: Enter the replacement HSA-id. The value must use the HSA-id format and differ from the current lead.
specification.responsibleChangeError: Could not change the specification lead.
specification.responsibleChangeInvalid: Check the new specification lead HSA-id.
specification.responsibleChangeSame: The new specification lead must differ from the current lead.
specification.responsibleChangeCoAuthorConflict: The specification lead cannot also be specification co-author.
specification.responsibleChangeUnsavedConfirm: You have unsaved changes in the specification. The lead change is saved separately and your unsaved changes are discarded if the form closes.
specification.coAuthorsHelp: Add verified people who can author content in this requirements specification.
specification.coAuthorHsaIdHelp: Enter the co-author HSA-id with two uppercase country-code letters, a 10-digit organization number, a hyphen, and an alphanumeric suffix.
specification.readOnlyNotice: You can read this specification, but you cannot change its content or assignments.
specification.forbiddenTitle: You do not have access to this requirements specification
specification.forbiddenBody: The requirements specification {uniqueId} ({name}) exists, but it is not assigned to you. Contact the specification lead to request access.
specification.implementationTypeHelp: Choose the implementation type that best describes how this specification will be delivered.
specification.lifecycleStatusHelp: Choose the specification lifecycle status that best describes the current phase of the specification.
specification.needsReferenceHelp: Describe why the requirement application is needed in the specification. The reference can also support when and how the requirement should be verified.
specification.deleteNeedsReferenceConfirm: Delete the needs reference "{needsReference}"? This is only possible while no requirements use it.
specification.deleteNeedsReferenceDisabled: Needs references used by requirements cannot be deleted.
specification.needsReferenceDescriptionHelp: Describe the need, decision, case, or source that this needs reference represents. Leave empty if it must be completed later.
specification.needsReferenceDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe the need this reference represents
specification.noNeedsReferences: No needs references have been created for this specification.
specification.noLinkedRequirements: No requirements use this needs reference.
specification.bulkNeedsReferenceHelp: Choose the needs reference to apply to every selected requirement in the specification, or choose no needs reference to clear the assignment.
specification.businessNeedsReferencePlaceholder: Free text describing why the specification exists or what capability it supports
specification.businessNeedsReferenceHelp: Use this field to capture the specification purpose, for example the business need, initiative, or capability this specification supports. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
specification.uniqueIdHelp: Used in URL and as short identifier
specification.uniqueIdGenerationFailed: Could not generate a specification ID from the name. Enter one manually.
specification.uniqueIdTaken: This Specification ID is already taken
specification.selectSpecificationHelp: Choose the requirements specification that should receive this requirement.
specification.removeConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove {count, plural, one {1 requirement} other {# requirements}} from this specification?
specification.removeSpecificationLocalConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete {count, plural, one {1 unique requirement} other {# unique requirements}} from this specification? They cannot be restored.
specification.removeMixedConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove {libraryCount, plural, one {1 library requirement} other {# library requirements}} from this specification and permanently delete {specificationLocalCount, plural, one {1 unique requirement} other {# unique requirements}}? Deleted unique requirements cannot be restored.
specification.noItems: No requirements are linked to this specification.
specification.localRequirementNotFound: Unique requirement not found in this specification.
specification.localRequirementActionDisabledTooltip: This unique requirement can only be edited or removed when Usage status is Included and no deviation is pending.
specification.graduateLocalRequirementTargetHelp: Choose the requirement area in the Requirements Library where the copied draft requirement should be created.
specification.graduateLocalRequirementConfirm: Create a new draft requirement in the Requirements Library from this unique requirement? The unique requirement stays in this specification.
specification.graduateLocalRequirementDisabledTooltip: This unique requirement can only be graduated when Usage status is Included.
specification.graduateLocalRequirementFailed: Could not graduate the unique requirement.
specification.deleteLocalRequirementConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete this unique requirement? It cannot be restored.
specification.addToSpecificationSuccess: The requirement has been added to the specification.
specification.alreadyInSpecification: This requirement is already in the specification.
specification.noPublishedVersionError: This requirement has no published version and cannot be added to a specification.
specification.requirementSelectionFilterDisabledToolti: The answered requirement selection questions do not provide a requirement selection. Answer a question with requirement-linked answers to filter the list.
specification.requirementSelectionNoPublishedMatches: The selected requirement-selection answers do not currently match any published requirements.

specification.loadSpecificationFailed: Could not load the requirements specification.
specification.loadSpecificationItemsFailed: Could not load requirements in the specification.
specification.partialDataLoadWarning: Some specification data could not be loaded. Existing data is still shown where available.
specification.noSpecificationsAvailable: There are no requirements specifications to add this requirement to. Create a specification first.
specification.saveFailed: Could not save the requirements specification.
specification.addNeedsRefHelp: Optionally apply a needs reference to all selected requirements when adding them to the specification.
specification.addNeedsRefTextHelp: Describe the shared needs reference that should be stored and linked to the selected requirements. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
specification.addNeedsRefPlaceholder: Enter a needs reference that applies to all selected requirements

requirementPackage.name: Name
requirementPackage.nameHelp: Enter the requirements package name. The name is authored in one language and shown for both Swedish and English until translation is introduced.
requirementPackage.nameSvHelp: Enter the Swedish name for this requirements package.
requirementPackage.nameEnHelp: Enter the English name for this requirements package.
requirementPackage.descriptionHelp: Optionally describe the grouping this requirements package represents. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
requirementPackage.descriptionSvHelp: Optionally describe the requirements package in Swedish so users understand the grouping it represents. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
requirementPackage.descriptionEnHelp: Optionally describe the requirements package in English so users understand the grouping it represents. Do not enter names or other details that can identify a living person.
requirementPackage.leadDisplayNameHelp: Enter the display name for the person responsible for this requirements package.
requirementPackage.leadHsaIdHelp: Enter the requirements package lead HSA-id with two uppercase country-code letters, a 10-digit organization number, a hyphen, and an alphanumeric suffix.
requirementPackage.createResponsibilityNotice: You become the requirements package lead when the package is created. That means you are responsible for the package's purpose, coherence, and keeping its included requirements relevant and current. Requirements package lead responsibility can be transferred to another person after agreement.
requirementPackage.changeLeadDescription: Enter the HSA-id that should lead this requirements package from now on.
requirementPackage.currentLeadHsaIdHelp: Shows the HSA-id currently assigned to the requirements package.
requirementPackage.newLeadHsaIdHelp: Enter the replacement HSA-id. The value must use the HSA-id format, differ from the previous lead, and must not be a package co-author.
requirementPackage.leadChangeError: Could not change requirements package lead.
requirementPackage.leadChangeCoAuthorConflict: The requirements package lead cannot also be requirements package co-author.
requirementPackage.leadChangeUnsavedConfirm: You have unsaved changes in this requirements package. If you change the requirements package lead now, the form will close and those changes will be lost.
requirementPackage.currentUserUnavailable: The signed-in user's HSA-id could not be loaded. Sign in again before creating a requirements package.
requirementPackage.coAuthorsHelp: Add HSA-id values for people who support the requirements package lead.
requirementPackage.coAuthorHsaIdHelp: Enter the co-author HSA-id with two uppercase country-code letters, a 10-digit organization number, a hyphen, and an alphanumeric suffix.

help.owner: Choose the owner responsible for this requirements package and its linked requirements.

normReference.filterByName: Filter by ID, name, type, reference, or issuer
normReference.issuerHelp: Specify the authority or organization that issued the document. Examples: Riksdagen, ISO, MSB, Myndigheten för digital förvaltning.
normReference.name: Name

help.name: Enter the official name of the governing document. Use the full, commonly recognized title. Example: General Data Protection Regulation, Informationssäkerhet för samhällsviktiga och digitala tjänster.

normReference.normReferenceIdHelp: A unique identifier for this norm reference. Leave blank to auto-generate from the reference field, name, or a sequential number. See the Norm library help panel for the full generation algorithm.
normReference.referenceHelp: Enter the formal document identifier or designation. Use the official numbering such as SFS 2018:218, ISO/IEC 27001:2022, or MSBFS 2020:6. This value is also used for auto-generating the norm reference ID.
normReference.requirementCount: {count, plural, =0 {0 requirements} one {1 requirement} other {# requirements}}
normReference.typeHelp: Classify the document type. Choose or type the document category: Law, Regulation, Directive, Standard, Guideline, or Strategic guideline. This helps filter and group norm references.
normReference.uriHelp: Optionally provide a web link to the official document. When a URI is set, the norm reference name becomes a clickable link in the requirement detail view.
normReference.versionHelp: Optionally specify the edition or version of the document. Use this to distinguish between revisions of the same document, for example 2022 or 3rd edition.
normReference.emptyState: No norm references yet. Create your first norm reference.

qualityCharacteristicMgmt.chapterIdHelp: Enter the ISO/IEC 25010 chapter number for this quality characteristic, for example 3.1 or 3.1.1.
qualityCharacteristicMgmt.name: Name
qualityCharacteristicMgmt.nameSvHelp: Enter the Swedish name for this quality characteristic.
qualityCharacteristicMgmt.nameEnHelp: Enter the English name for this quality characteristic.
qualityCharacteristicMgmt.typeHelp: Choose the requirement type this quality characteristic belongs to.
qualityCharacteristicMgmt.parentHelp: Optionally choose a top-level characteristic to create a sub-characteristic.
qualityCharacteristicMgmt.subtitle: ISO/IEC 25010:2023 — Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)

statusMgmt.name: Name
statusMgmt.nameSvHelp: Enter the Swedish requirement version status name shown when the application language is Swedish.
statusMgmt.nameEnHelp: Enter the English requirement version status name shown when the application language is English.
statusMgmt.colorHelp: Choose the color used for this requirement version status badge.
statusMgmt.iconHelp: Choose an approved icon shown next to the requirement version status label. Leave empty for a text-only badge.
statusMgmt.sortOrderHelp: Enter a number that controls the display order. Lower numbers appear first.
statusMgmt.cannotDeleteSystem: System requirement version statuses cannot be deleted
statusMgmt.cannotDeleteInUse: Cannot delete — requirement version status is used by existing requirements
statusMgmt.emptyState: No requirement version statuses are available.

admin.title: Admin center
admin.description: Manage core admin settings for the system, including columns, taxonomy, statuses and workflows, access reviews, and privacy-related configuration.
admin.adminCenterDescription: Update default column settings and admin settings.
admin.columnsDescription: Control the default order and visibility of the requirements list columns.

identity.title: Identity
identity.description: Manage the HSA-id prefixes that are offered when users enter HSA-id values.
identity.defaultMustBeVisible: The default HSA-id prefix must be visible.
identity.defaultRequired: Select one visible HSA-id prefix as the default.
identity.duplicatePrefix: Each HSA-id prefix must be unique.
identity.emptyPrefixes: No HSA-id prefixes are configured. HSA-id suffix fields stay locked until an administrator adds a visible prefix.

fieldHelp.defaultPrefix: The star marks the prefix preselected in new empty HSA-id fields. Selecting a hidden prefix as default also makes it visible.
fieldHelp.label: Optionally add a short display label. Users see the label together with the prefix in HSA-id prefix lists.
fieldHelp.prefix: Enter the part before the hyphen in an HSA-id: two uppercase country-code letters followed by ten digits.
fieldHelp.visible: The eye controls whether the prefix is offered in HSA-id prefix lists. Hide a used prefix when it should remain valid for existing assignments but no longer be offered for new ones. A non-empty list must always have at least one visible prefix.

identity.invalidPrefix: Enter each HSA-id prefix as two uppercase letters followed by ten digits.
identity.label: Label
identity.usedPrefix: This prefix has been used in an HSA-id and can be hidden, but not removed.
identity.usedPrefixDeleteBlocked: Used HSA-id prefixes cannot be removed. Hide the prefix instead.
identity.visibleRequired: At least one HSA-id prefix must be visible.

admin.identityDescription: Manage HSA-id prefixes for editable responsibility fields.
admin.taxonomyDescription: Open classification pages used for filtering, reporting, and AI support.
admin.statusesAndWorkflowsDescription: Open status catalogs that control life cycles, workflow steps, and requirement use in specifications.
admin.accessReviewDescription: Create recurring reviews of app-managed assignments, decide each access row, and export review evidence.
admin.privacyDescription: Preview HSA-id based matches, export data, and execute GDPR erasure for actor identity snapshots and live assignments.

auditLog.title: Action log
auditLog.description: Search application mutations and authorization denials recorded in the database.
auditLog.empty: No action log events match the filters.

privacy.title: Privacy

fieldHelp.targetHsaId: Enter the HSA-id to search for before preview, export, or erasure. Every match uses the exact HSA-id. Names are not used for matching, so there is no separate name search field.
fieldHelp.replacementHsaId: Enter the HSA-id for the person who should take over live assignments, such as owner, co-author, or specification lead. This field must be combined with replacement name before the Switch action can be used.
fieldHelp.replacementName: Enter the display name snapshot to store for the replacement person. The name helps the operator confirm the switch, but HSA-id remains the durable identity.
fieldHelp.replacementFirstName: Optional. Enter the exact first-name value for the owner record when the display name cannot be split reliably.
fieldHelp.replacementLastName: Optional. Enter the exact last-name value for the owner record when the display name cannot be split reliably.
fieldHelp.replacementEmail: Optional. Enter an email address when a replacement owner should be created or updated so requirement areas and packages keep the right contact details after the switch.

privacy.disabledTooltip: You need the Privacy officer permission.
privacy.permissionError: You need the Privacy officer role. Sign in as a privacy officer to run privacy erasure.
privacy.replacementIncomplete: Enter both replacement HSA-id and replacement name, or leave both fields empty to anonymize.
privacy.invalidHsaId: Check the HSA-id. The format is two uppercase country-code letters, 10 digits, a hyphen, and an alphanumeric suffix.
privacy.serverPreviewError: The server could not preview privacy erasure.
privacy.serverExecuteError: The server could not execute privacy erasure.
privacy.stalePreview: The preview is stale. Run preview again before executing.
privacy.confirmMessage: This will switch {switch}, anonymize {anonymize}, delete {delete}, and skip {skip} occurrence(s).
privacy.guidanceBody: Use the preview to verify exactly which HSA-id matches can be exported or erased. Export does not change the data. Live assignments, including specification leads, are usually switched to another HSA-id during erasure. Historical creator, decision, and resolution fields are usually anonymized instead of reassigned. Description and free-text fields are not scanned because the UI instructs users not to enter person-identifying details there.

executionErrors.owner_area_references_blocking: Requirement areas must be switched before the owner can be changed.
executionErrors.owner_references_blocking: Owner references must be switched before the owner can be deleted.
executionErrors.replacement_required: Replacement HSA-id and replacement name are required for the selected action.
executionErrors.unsupported_owner_action: The owner row does not support the selected action.
executionErrors.unsupported_privacy_action: The row does not support the selected action.

warnings.ownerSwitch: Switch references to a replacement owner, or anonymize to the deleted-user sentinel when no replacement is supplied.
warnings.ownerDelete: This owner is not assigned to requirement areas or packages. Delete the owner row, or skip it.
warnings.ownerAreaSwitchOnly: This owner is assigned to requirement areas. Switch those assignments to the replacement owner, or skip the owner row.
warnings.ownerPackageSwitchOnly: This owner is assigned to requirement packages. Switch those assignments to the replacement owner, or skip the owner row.
warnings.ownerAreaAndPackageSwitchOnly: This owner is assigned to requirement areas and packages. Switch those assignments to the replacement owner, or skip the owner row.
warnings.liveAssignment: Live assignments normally switch to the replacement HSA-id. Without a replacement, remove co-author rows or keep the anonymized owner.
warnings.historySwitch: Switching historical authorship can change the record's meaning. Anonymize is the default.
warnings.decisionSwitch: Decisions and resolutions should not be reassigned unless legally documented. Anonymize is the default.

blockers.ownerAreaReplacementRequired: This owner is assigned to requirement areas. Update those areas first or specify a replacement HSA-id and name.
blockers.ownerPackageReplacementRequired: This owner is assigned to requirement packages. Update those packages first or specify a replacement HSA-id and name.
blockers.ownerAreaAndPackageReplacementRequired: This owner is assigned to requirement areas and packages. Update those assignments first or specify a replacement HSA-id and name.

readOnly.controlledByOwner: Shown for information. Requirement areas and packages are switched automatically when the owner row is switched.

archiving.body: Use archiving to preview and execute approved retention policies and export archive evidence before deletion. The data protection flow remains separate for data-subject cases.
archiving.description: Preview retention candidates, manage exceptions, and export archive material before data is removed from the application.
archiving.disabledTooltip: You need the Privacy officer role.
archiving.heading: Archiving and retention

retention.confirmMessage: This will process {archiveCount} archive row(s) and delete {deleteCount} row(s).
retention.defaultExceptionReason: Exempted from archiving preview by Privacy officer.
retention.description: Retention and archiving are handled here. De-identification belongs in the export, while the application database only executes approved retention actions.
retention.exportRequired: Export the archive evidence before running retention.
retention.stalePreview: The retention preview is stale. Run preview again.
retention.title: Retention

archiving.title: Archiving

accessReview.title: Access review
accessReview.disabledTooltip: You need the Administrator or Privacy officer role.
accessReview.cancelConfirmMessage: Review #{id} will be marked as cancelled. The review rows are kept as history, but can no longer be decided or completed.
accessReview.commentTooLong: The comment can be at most {max} characters.
accessReview.createBlockedByOpenRun: An access review is already in progress. Complete or cancel it before creating a new one.
accessReview.decisionError: Could not save the access review decision.
accessReview.externalEvidenceHelp: This field is optional and points to external evidence for permissions that Kravhantering does not own, such as IdP role review for the Administrator, Reviewer, and Privacy officer roles, source-code repository access, and external MCP/client provisioning. Enter a ticket ID, audit-report link, or case/document number. The external evidence should show period, scope, systems/roles reviewed, reviewer, decisions, actions, and sign-off.
accessReview.externalEvidenceNote: Global IdP roles, source-code access, and external MCP client provisioning are reviewed in the tools where those permissions are assigned.
accessReview.itemsDescription: Rows are snapshots from app-managed assignments at the time the review was created. Click the row icon to mark it complete. When all rows are marked complete, the review can be completed.

admin.columnsSaveError: Could not save the column settings.
admin.areasDescription: Manage requirement areas and their prefixes.
admin.categoriesDescription: View requirement categories used to classify requirement perspectives.
admin.typesDescription: View types and their quality hierarchy.
admin.requirementPackagesDescription: Manage requirements packages linked to requirements.
admin.statusesDescription: Manage requirement version statuses and their ordering.
admin.governanceObjectTypesDescription: Manage governance object types for requirements specifications.
admin.implementationTypesDescription: Manage implementation types for requirements specifications.
admin.riskLevelsDescription: Manage risk levels that can be assigned to requirements.
admin.specificationItemStatusesDescription: Manage usage statuses for requirement applications.

graduationTargets.title: Requirements Library target requirement areas
graduationTargets.summary: {count} requirement area(s) can receive the copy.

graduate.title: Unique Requirement Graduated to Requirements Library
graduate.summary: Unique requirement {sourceUniqueId} was copied to {requirementUniqueId} as a draft in {targetAreaName}.

footer.copyright: © {year} Contributors to Requirements Management. All rights reserved.

overview.heading: Overview
overview.body: The requirements library is the main workspace for finding, tracking, and opening requirements. It shows every requirement you have access to, its current state, and lets you continue working without leaving the list.

inlineDetail.heading: Inline detail view
inlineDetail.body: Click a row to open its detail view directly underneath. There you can read the requirement text, acceptance criteria, metadata, references, requirements packages, status steps, and version history. If the requirement has a published version, that version is normally shown first; otherwise an archived version is shown, and otherwise the latest working version. The buttons on the right only show the actions that are available for the version and status you are currently viewing.

properties.heading: Requirement properties
properties.body: Each requirement carries a set of properties that describe what it demands, how it is classified, and where it fits in the organization's processes. Below is an overview of every property and how it supports the requirement lifecycle.

requirementId.heading: Requirement ID
requirementId.body: Generated automatically from the requirement area prefix and a sequence number. This is the stable identifier used for traceability across requirements specifications, reports, and cross-references. You cannot edit it directly — it is assigned when the requirement is created.

area.heading: Requirement area
area.body: Groups requirements by domain, such as Integration or Case Management. The requirement area determines the ID prefix and which team is responsible for the requirement. It must be set when creating a new requirement and cannot be changed afterward.

description.heading: Requirement text
description.body: The core statement of what must be fulfilled. This is the main content that stakeholders review during the Review phase and that becomes binding once the requirement is published. Keep it clear and testable.

acceptanceCriteria.heading: Acceptance criteria
acceptanceCriteria.body: Measurable conditions that must be met for the requirement to be considered fulfilled. They are essential during verification and testing, and help requirement reviewers understand the precise intent behind the requirement text.

category.heading: Category
category.body: Classifies the requirement as Business, IT, or Supplier. This supports filtering in the library and makes it easier to assign responsibility to the right team or stakeholder group.

type.heading: Type
type.body: Indicates whether the requirement is Functional or Non-functional. Both types unlock the quality characteristic field, but non-functional requirements offer a wider set of characteristics from the ISO/IEC 25010 standard while functional requirements have a smaller subset.

qualityCharacteristic.heading: Quality characteristic
qualityCharacteristic.body: Follows the ISO/IEC 25010 standard and lets you classify requirements by qualities such as Security, Performance efficiency, or Usability. Non-functional requirements have access to the full set of characteristics, while functional requirements offer a smaller subset. This helps prioritize and group related requirements during review and implementation planning.

riskLevel.heading: Risk level
riskLevel.body: Marks the requirement as Low, Medium, or High risk. Higher risk levels signal that the requirement needs more thorough review, more rigorous testing, and closer follow-up during implementation. The risk level also plays a role when a deviation is requested within a requirements specification — a deviation for a high-risk requirement carries greater potential impact and therefore demands stricter scrutiny during the deviation review.

verifiable.heading: Verifiable
verifiable.body: Indicates whether the requirement can be tested. When you enable this toggle, you must also specify a verification method. The verification method is used within requirements specifications where the requirement is included, for example in connection with a procurement, implementation, or management process.

verificationMethod.heading: Verification method
verificationMethod.body: Describes exactly how the requirement will be verified — for example through automated tests, manual inspection, or user acceptance testing. This field is required when Verifiable is turned on. The method is applied within the requirements specifications where the requirement is used, providing concrete guidance for how verification should be carried out in each context.

requirementPackages.heading: Requirements packages
requirementPackages.body: Groups requirements for a specific area of concern, such as mobile use, data migration, integrations with other systems, case management, usability, cloud operations, normal operations, high load, or disaster recovery. Each requirements package has an owner responsible for the requirements in that grouping. Linking packages helps scope specifications and filter requirements by the groupings that matter for a delivery.

normReferences.heading: Norm references
normReferences.body: Connects the requirement to external standards, laws, or regulations. These links support compliance auditing and make it possible to trace every requirement back to its legal or normative origin.

status.heading: Requirement version status
status.body: Reflects the current requirement version status — Draft, Review, Published, or Archived. The status is managed automatically through workflow actions and controls which operations are available. See the Process illustration and Requirement process sections below for details.

filtering.heading: Searching and filtering
filtering.body: Use the search field for free-text searches in requirement IDs and content. Click the filter icon on any column header to narrow the list to the right requirement area, requirement version status, or other metadata. Turn on "Show archived" when you need to include requirements that are no longer active.

columns.heading: Columns
columns.body: The Columns button controls which metadata columns are visible in the table. Drag column edges to resize them. The Requirement ID and Requirement text columns are always visible so you can stay oriented even when you customize the view.

lifecycleVisual.heading: Process illustration
lifecycleVisual.body: This visual shows the main path a requirement follows, plus the points where work loops back to Draft or Published instead of continuing straight forward.

draft.title: Draft
draft.description: The requirement is being written or revised.

review.title: Review
review.description: Stakeholders review the content before publication.

published.title: Published
published.description: This is the published requirement version that currently applies.

archivingReview.title: Archiving review
archivingReview.description: Archiving has started but can still be approved or cancelled.

archived.title: Archived
archived.description: The requirement is retired and read-only.

transitions.editCreatesDraft: Edit: creates a new Draft while Published stays active

lifecycle.heading: Requirement process
lifecycle.body: Draft: you can write, edit, delete the latest draft, and send it to review. Review: the content is locked for editing until you send it back to Draft or publish it. Published: this is the published requirement version; you can add it to specifications, share or print it, and start editing, which creates a new draft while the published requirement version stays in effect. When you choose Archive, the requirement first enters an archiving review where you can approve or cancel the archiving. Archived: the requirement is read-only and cannot be edited or added to specifications; restore it to create a new draft.

actions.heading: Actions
actions.body: Use the toolbar to create a new requirement, export the visible list to CSV or PDF, or print it. Select rows with the checkboxes when you want to add multiple requirements to a requirements specification or generate a combined review report.

form.heading: Filling in the form
form.body: Select a requirement area first — it determines the requirement ID prefix and the responsible owner. Each field with a question-mark icon has inline guidance. Required fields are marked.

lifecycle.heading: Lifecycle
lifecycle.body: A newly created requirement starts as a Draft. It must be submitted for review and then published before it can be added to a requirements specification.

status.heading: Status transitions
status.body: Use the requirement version status bar to advance the requirement through its lifecycle: Draft → Review → Published. To archive, the requirement goes through an archiving review: Published → Review (archiving) → Archived. Each transition requires a confirmation. You can return to Draft from Review if more work is needed.

versions.heading: Versions
versions.body: Every approved change creates a new version. Use the Version History section to compare versions or restore an earlier one. Restoring creates a new Draft based on the selected version.

reports.heading: Reports
reports.body: Generate a review report or history report for this requirement using the report buttons. Reports are available as print view or PDF download.

versioning.heading: Versioning
versioning.body: Editing a Published requirement creates a new Draft version. The published version remains active until the draft completes the review process and is published.

form.heading: Saving
form.body: Use "After save, go to" to choose where you land after saving — the list view or the detail page. Changes are not auto-saved; click Save to commit them.

overview.heading: Viewing a specific version
overview.body: This page shows the requirement as it was at a specific version. Use the navigation at the top to move between versions.

restore.heading: Restoring this version
restore.body: Click Restore to create a new Draft based on this version's content. The current published version remains active until the new draft is reviewed and published.

overview.heading: What is a requirements specification?
overview.body: Specifications group published requirements for a specific delivery, procurement, or business context. Each specification can be linked to a governance object type, an implementation type, and a specification lead.

create.heading: Creating and editing
create.body: Click "New requirements specification" to create one. Click the specification name to open its detail view. Use the edit icon to update the specification metadata, including the specification lead.

requirements.heading: Managing requirements
requirements.body: Click "Add requirements" to open the picker and add published requirements to this specification. Select one or more rows and click "Remove selected" to remove them.

needsReference.heading: Needs reference
needsReference.body: Each requirement application in the specification can have a needs reference — free text that explains why the requirement is needed in this specific specification. It can also support when the requirement should be verified. Set it when adding requirements or edit it inline.

export.heading: Exporting
export.body: Use the print and PDF buttons to export the specification's requirement list as a report.

specificationItemStatus.heading: Usage status
specificationItemStatus.body: Track how far each requirement has progressed within this specification: included, in progress, implemented, verified, or not applicable. Change the status inline in the table.

overview.heading: Purpose
overview.body: Requirement selection questions capture the delivery context as selection context for the specification. When Filter with requirement selection questions is on, answered questions narrow the available requirements list.

filtering.heading: Finding questions
filtering.body: Use the area filter, unanswered-only toggle, and search field to focus the list. The progress summary shows how many active questions have answers.

answers.heading: Choosing answers
answers.body: Choose the answer or answers that fit the specification. Some answers match published requirements directly, while others match requirements through requirements packages.

saving.heading: Saving choices
saving.body: Each answer is saved when you select it. Clearing a question removes its saved answers and makes the question unanswered again.

historical.heading: Historical choices
historical.body: Inactive or archived questions and answers are shown as historical choices when they were saved earlier. Historical choices are preserved for traceability but do not affect current matching.

overview.heading: What are governance object types?
overview.body: Governance object types identify the type of governance context a requirements specification belongs to, such as management object, delivery area, service area, project, or assignment.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Click the edit icon on a row to rename a governance object type. Use the delete icon to remove it — only governance object types not linked to any specification can be deleted.

overview.heading: What are implementation types?
overview.body: Implementation types describe how a requirements specification will be delivered, for example procurement, in-house development, or integration. Assign one when creating or editing a specification.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Click the edit icon to rename an implementation type. Use the delete icon to remove it — only types not linked to any specification can be deleted.

overview.heading: What are specification lifecycle statuses?
overview.body: Specification lifecycle statuses describe the current phase of a requirements specification, for example procurement, implementation, development, or management. Assign one when creating or editing a specification.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Click the edit icon to rename a specification lifecycle status. Use the delete icon to remove it — only specification lifecycle statuses not linked to any specification can be deleted.

columns.heading: Default columns
columns.body: Set which columns appear by default in the requirements library and in what order. Users can still override column visibility per session. Locked columns are always shown.

identity.heading: Identity settings
identity.body: Manage HSA-id prefixes used by editable HSA-id fields. Prefixes are UI guidance only; server-side HSA-id validation still accepts every syntactically valid HSA-id.

taxonomy.heading: Taxonomy
taxonomy.body: Quick links to classifications used for filtering, reporting, and AI support: requirement areas, categories, types, risk levels, quality characteristics, governance object types, and implementation types.

statusesAndWorkflows.heading: Statuses and workflows
statusesAndWorkflows.body: Quick links to status catalogs that control lifecycle and usage states: requirement version statuses, specification lifecycle statuses, and usage statuses.

privacy.heading: Privacy erasure
privacy.body: Use the Privacy tab for GDPR erasure work. Matches are made by HSA-id only; names are shown for confirmation and never create matches.

accessReview.heading: Access review
accessReview.body: Use Access review to create recurring review rounds for app-managed assignments. Global IdP roles are reviewed in the identity-management tool.

archiving.heading: Archiving and retention
archiving.body: Use the Archiving tab to preview and run approved retention rules and export archive evidence before data is removed. Privacy erasure is handled separately.

overview.heading: Purpose
overview.body: The Identity tab controls which HSA-id prefixes are offered when users fill in editable HSA-id fields. The prefixes are UI guidance, not server-side policy.

prefixes.heading: Prefixes and labels
prefixes.body: Enter the prefix as the part before the hyphen in an HSA-id. The label is optional and only helps users recognize choices in the list.

visibility.heading: Visibility
visibility.body: Visible prefixes appear in users' prefix lists. Hide a prefix when it should remain on existing assignments but no longer be offered for new HSA-id values. A non-empty list must always have at least one visible prefix.

defaultPrefix.heading: Default prefix
defaultPrefix.body: The default prefix is preselected in new empty HSA-id fields. It must be visible; selecting a hidden prefix as default also makes it visible.

overview.heading: Purpose
overview.body: The Privacy tab is used when an authorized officer needs to preview and execute GDPR erasure for actor identities. It handles structured identity fields such as requirement area owner HSA-id values, package leads, creators, decision makers, and co-authors. Free-text fields are not scanned because users are instructed not to enter person-identifying details there.

permissions.heading: Permission
permissions.body: The workflow requires the Privacy officer role. The role is intentionally narrow: it grants access to this privacy workflow but should not grant unrelated administrative powers.

search.heading: Search by HSA-id
search.body: Start by entering the person's HSA-id. Every match uses the exact HSA-id, not the name. That prevents two people with the same name from being mixed together. Names are shown only as confirmation support in the preview.

replacement.heading: Replacement
replacement.body: Enter a replacement HSA-id when live assignments should move to another person. If no replacement is supplied, historical identity fields are anonymized where allowed.

preview.heading: Preview
preview.body: Run Preview before executing. The table groups matches by object type and field, showing how many records are affected, the current display value, affected objects, and the recommended action.

actions.heading: Table actions
actions.body: Switch is used for live assignments when a replacement is supplied. Anonymize is usually used for historical creators, decisions, and resolutions. Delete is used only when an identity row can be removed without breaking references. Skip leaves the row unchanged. Some rows are locked to prevent invalid combinations.

execution.heading: When you execute erasure
execution.body: When you click Execute erasure, the server checks the matches again inside a transaction. If the preview is stale, you must preview again. After a successful run, the table remains as a receipt: completed rows are green, skipped rows are neutral, and row-specific failures are shown in red.

audit.heading: Traceability
audit.body: The system writes a security audit event to the platform log stream with the handler identity, request id, action counts, and a non-reversible target fingerprint. That log is not a database table, is not shown in the preview, and is not rewritten by this workflow. The raw HSA-id and name for the erased person are not logged.

caveats.heading: Important limits
caveats.body: The officer must confirm that erasure is allowed and that no stronger legal basis or retention duty prevents the action. Security logs and external logs are handled by a separate retention or deletion policy. Anonymizing handler identity in those logs can reduce traceability.

overview.heading: Purpose
overview.body: Access review records who currently holds app-managed assignments, who reviewed them, and which decision was made for each row.

scope.heading: Scope
scope.body: The app inventories requirement area owners, requirement area co-authors, requirement package leads, requirement package co-authors, specification leads, and specification co-authors. Global IdP roles, source-code access, and external client provisioning are reviewed in their source tools.

decisions.heading: Decisions
decisions.body: Each row starts as pending and can be approved, marked for revocation, marked as changed, or marked not applicable. A review can be completed only when no rows are pending.

evidence.heading: Evidence
evidence.body: Export JSON as the structured evidence format. PDF is a readable rendering of the same payload. Store references to external IdP or repository access reviews in the evidence field.

description.heading: What is included
description.body: The export contains structured personal data that is connected to your verified HSA-id, such as owner records, creator snapshots, decisions, co-author assignments, and current session claims.

formats.heading: JSON and PDF
formats.body: JSON is the authoritative machine-readable export format. PDF is a readable rendering of the same payload for review or filing.

limits.heading: Important limits
limits.body: Free-text fields are not scanned because the product instructs users not to enter person-identifying data there. Platform security-audit logs are handled in the operational log stream and are not included in this application export.

overview.heading: What are requirement areas?
overview.body: Requirement areas define the ID prefix for requirements (e.g. PERF-001) and assign a responsible owner. A requirement must belong to a requirement area before it can be saved.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Create a new requirement area using the New button. Click the edit icon to update the prefix, name, description, or owner. Deleting a requirement area is only possible if no requirements are assigned to it.

overview.heading: What are categories?
overview.body: Categories classify the requirement perspective, for example business, IT, or supplier requirements. They are part of the taxonomy used for filtering and reporting.

boundary.heading: Read-only catalog
boundary.body: Categories are maintained as fixed reference data in this slice. Admin Center shows them for orientation, but they are not created, edited, or deleted here.

overview.heading: Functional vs. non-functional
overview.body: Types classify requirements. Functional requirements describe behaviors the system must perform. Non-functional requirements describe quality properties such as performance, security, or usability.

quality.heading: Quality characteristics
quality.body: The type controls which quality characteristics can be linked to a requirement. Non-functional types can be associated with the ISO/IEC 25010:2023 quality characteristic hierarchy.

overview.heading: What are requirements packages?
overview.body: Requirements packages group requirements for a specific area of concern, such as mobile use, data migration, integrations with other systems, case management, usability, cloud operations, normal operations, high load, or disaster recovery.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Create and edit requirements packages using the toolbar. A requirements package can be linked to multiple requirements. Removing a package only removes the package record; linked requirements are not affected.

overview.heading: Purpose
overview.body: Use this stewardship view to maintain the questions that narrow available requirements in a specification. Questions belong to requirement areas and are managed as Requirements Library content.

answers.heading: Answers and matching
answers.body: Add answer options that point to published requirements directly, to requirements packages, or to an intentional no-selection answer. Active answers influence filtering when users choose them in a specification.

visibility.heading: Visibility
visibility.body: Use the visibility panel on a question to make it conditional on earlier requirement selection answers. A question with no visibility condition is shown independently of answers to other requirement selection questions. A conditional question is shown when at least one condition group matches.

health.heading: Health indicators
health.body: Answers that no longer match any published requirements are marked as missing a requirement selection. Review those answers after packages or requirement versions are archived. Inactive questions or answers can remain in visibility conditions, but they do not match and are called out in the visibility panel.

overview.heading: What are norm references?
overview.body:
Norm references are the governing documents — laws, regulations, ordinances, standards, and guidelines — that a requirement is derived from or must comply with. Examples: the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), ISO/IEC 27001:2022, or MSBFS 2020:6.

A norm reference is a shared object in the system and can be linked to any number of requirements. This means that updating a norm reference affects all requirements linked to it.

idGeneration.heading: How the Norm Reference ID is auto-generated
idGeneration.body:
If you leave the ID field blank, an ID is generated automatically using the following priority order:

1. Natural ID from the reference field — the system tries to extract a structured ID from the reference field. Example: "SFS 2018:218" produces SFS-2018-218 and "ISO 27001:2022" produces ISO-27001-2022.
2. Slug from the name — if no natural ID can be derived, the name is used as a base. The text is converted to uppercase, diacritics are normalized, and the result is truncated to 20 characters. Example: "General Data Protection Regulation" gives GENERAL-DATA-PROTECTI.
3. Sequence as a last resort — if neither method yields a usable ID, a sequential ID is assigned in the form NR-001, NR-002, and so on.
4. Collision handling — if the generated ID already exists, a suffix is appended: -2, -3, etc., until a unique ID is found.

manage.heading: Managing norm references
manage.body:
Create norm references using the Create button or directly from the requirement form. Fill in name, type (e.g. Law, Standard), reference, optional version, and issuer.

A norm reference cannot be deleted if it is linked to one or more requirements — remove the links from the relevant requirements first. Linked requirements are shown in the table when editing a norm reference.

overview.heading: Requirement version statuses
overview.body: Requirement version statuses represent the lifecycle stages a requirement version passes through: Draft, Review, Published, and Archived. These four system requirement version statuses cannot be deleted or renamed.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: You can reorder and configure the color of requirement version statuses using the controls in each row. System requirement version statuses are marked and cannot be removed.

overview.heading: ISO/IEC 25010:2023 hierarchy
overview.body: Quality characteristics are organized in a two-level tree following ISO/IEC 25010:2023 SQuaRE. Top-level characteristics (e.g. Performance efficiency) can have sub-characteristics (e.g. Time behaviour).

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Add new characteristics or sub-characteristics using the New button. A sub-characteristic must be assigned to a parent. Characteristics linked to existing requirements cannot be deleted.

overview.heading: About risk levels
overview.body: Risk levels indicate the potential impact if a requirement is not met. Each level has a name, color, and sort order.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Risk levels are a fixed system set. Edit existing levels to change name, color, icon, or sort order.

overview.heading: About usage statuses
overview.body: Usage statuses track how far a requirement has progressed within a specification — from included to implemented and verified. Each usage status has a name, color, and sort order.

manage.heading: Managing
manage.body: Add new usage statuses using the Create button. Edit existing usage statuses to change name, color, or sort order. Usage statuses linked to requirement applications or unique requirements cannot be deleted.

riskLevelAdmin.name: Name
riskLevelAdmin.requirementCount: {count, plural, =0 {0 requirements} one {1 requirement} other {# requirements}}

specificationItemStatusAdmin.title: Usage statuses
specificationItemStatusAdmin.name: Name
specificationItemStatusAdmin.iconHelp: Choose an approved icon shown next to this usage status label. Leave empty for a text-only badge.
specificationItemStatusAdmin.itemCount: {count, plural, =0 {0 items} one {1 item} other {# items}}
specificationItemStatusAdmin.requirementCount: {count, plural, =0 {0 requirements} one {1 requirement} other {# requirements}}
specificationItemStatusAdmin.nameSvHelp: Enter the Swedish name for this usage status. It is displayed when the application language is Swedish.
specificationItemStatusAdmin.nameEnHelp: Enter the English name for this usage status. It is displayed when the application language is English.
specificationItemStatusAdmin.definitionSvHelp: Enter a Swedish definition that describes what this usage status means. It is shown as a tooltip where the usage status is used.
specificationItemStatusAdmin.definitionEnHelp: Enter an English definition that describes what this usage status means. It is shown as a tooltip where the usage status is used.
specificationItemStatusAdmin.colorHelp: Choose the color used for this usage status badge.
specificationItemStatusAdmin.sortOrderHelp: Enter a number that controls the display order. Lower numbers appear first. Reserved usage statuses keep a locked sort order.
specificationItemStatusAdmin.sortOrderLocked: The sort order for this system usage status cannot be changed.

ai.topicPlaceholder: Describe the system or feature you want to generate requirements for...
ai.createConfirm: {count, plural, one {Create # requirement in requirement area {area}?} other {Create # requirements in requirement area {area}?}}
ai.closeConfirm: You have unsaved work. Are you sure you want to close and lose your progress?
ai.capabilityReasoningTooltip: The model supports chain-of-thought reasoning, showing its thinking process before answering. Required for high-quality requirement generation.
ai.capabilityStreamingTooltip: Responses are delivered incrementally as they are generated, so you can see reasoning and results in real time.
ai.capabilityStructuredOutputsTooltip: The model guarantees its output conforms to a strict JSON schema. When enabled, this replaces the default JSON output mode for more reliable structured results.
ai.capabilityToolsTooltip: The model can invoke external tools or functions during generation. Not required for requirement generation but may improve results with some models.
ai.capabilityResponseFormatTooltip: The model supports returning structured JSON output. This is required so generated requirements can be parsed and imported. If Strict JSON schema is enabled, it replaces this mode.
ai.capabilityVisionTooltip: The model can analyze images such as architecture diagrams, wireframes, or screenshots. When enabled, you can attach images as context for requirement generation.
ai.modelsMatch: {count, plural, one {# model matches} other {# models match}}
ai.dataPolicyProviderNote: Data privacy controls how AI providers handle your prompts and responses — whether they store, train on, or retain your data. These settings filter providers at request time and may reduce available providers for the selected model.
ai.dataPolicyDenyTrainingTooltip: Prevents providers from using your prompts and completions for training. The request is routed only to providers that do not collect data.
ai.dataPolicyZdrTooltip: Routes to providers that commit to zero data retention: prompts and completions are not stored after the request completes.
ai.dataPolicyDistillableTooltip: Distillable text means the completion is returned as plain, readable text that can be used to fine-tune (distill) other models. This setting blocks providers that only return opaque or encrypted outputs which cannot be reused for training.
ai.orgCreditsMissingKey: Set OPENROUTER_MGMT_API_KEY to show organization credits
ai.creditsErrorTooltip: Could not fetch credit balance. Check that OPENROUTER_API_KEY is valid and the API is reachable. Error: {detail}
ai.topicHelp: Describe the system or feature you want requirements for. The AI uses this as context when generating requirements.
ai.areaHelp: Choose which requirement area the generated requirements will be placed in. This determines where they appear in the requirements list.
ai.modelHelp: Select the AI model to use. Larger models produce higher-quality results but take longer to generate.

prompt.defaultInstruction:
Generate 5-10 well-structured system requirements for the given topic.

Each requirement MUST:
- Have a clear, testable, unambiguous description (per ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 §5.2.5)
- Use "The system shall..." form for functional requirements
- Use "The system shall be able to..." or quality-attribute language for non-functional requirements
- Include acceptance criteria where applicable
- Reference appropriate quality characteristics from ISO/IEC 25010:2023
- Include a rationale explaining why the requirement exists

For non-functional requirements, apply the ISO/IEC 25010:2023 quality model:
- Map each to the most specific quality sub-characteristic
- Consider performance efficiency, security, usability, reliability, and maintainability

Assess risk level based on:
- Impact on system safety, security, or core functionality (High)
- Impact on user experience or non-critical features (Medium)
- Nice-to-have or cosmetic requirements (Low)

system.intro:
You are an expert requirements engineer. You generate system requirements following international standards:
- ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 (Requirements engineering)
- ISO/IEC 25030:2019 (Quality requirements framework)
- ISO/IEC 25010:2023 (Quality model)
system.taxonomyIntro: You MUST use the following taxonomy IDs in your output. Only use IDs from the lists below.
outputRules: Output valid JSON matching the provided schema
Use exact IDs from the taxonomy above
typeId is required for every requirement
qualityCharacteristicId is recommended for non-functional requirements
categoryId should be set when the category is clear
requirementPackageIds must be [] or only contain IDs from the requirements packages list above
requiresTesting must be true for functional requirements and security requirements
verificationMethod should describe how to verify the requirement when requiresTesting is true
rationale must explain why the requirement matters for the system

ai.customInstructionHelp: Override the default instruction sent to the AI. Use this to steer the style, scope, or format of the generated requirements.
ai.reasoningEffortHelp: Control how much reasoning the model performs. Higher effort produces better results but uses more tokens and takes longer. Extra high allocates ~95% of tokens to reasoning, Off disables it entirely.
ai.streamClosedUnexpectedly: The response stream closed unexpectedly. Please try again.
ai.imageAttachHelp: Attach up to 3 images (PNG, JPEG, GIF or WebP, max 10 MB each) for the AI model to analyze. The selected model must support Vision. Images can consume 1,000+ tokens each, which increases generation cost.
ai.imageAttachHint: PNG, JPEG, GIF or WebP — max 10 MB each, up to 3 images
ai.imageDropZone: Drop images here or click to browse
ai.imageTokenWarning: Images can consume 1,000+ tokens each, which increases generation cost.
ai.imageErrorType: Unsupported file type: {name}. Use PNG, JPEG, GIF or WebP.
ai.imageErrorCount: You can attach up to {max} images.
ai.imageErrorRead: Failed to read one or more image files. Please try again.
